—
days
—
Jan 1 – Mar 31

This countdown provides estimates only and is not an official Medicare determination. Dates can vary by individual circumstances. Confirm with Medicare.gov, Social Security, or a licensed agent.

Medicare Enrollment Periods

Initial Enrollment Period (IEP)

A 7-month window around your 65th birthday — the 3 months before your birthday month, your birthday month, and the 3 months after.

Annual Enrollment Period (AEP)

October 15 – December 7 each year. Join, switch, or drop a Medicare Advantage or Part D plan for the following year.

General Enrollment Period (GEP)

January 1 – March 31, for people who missed their Initial Enrollment Period. Late-enrollment penalties may apply.

Special Enrollment Period (SEP)

If you have active employer coverage from a company with 20+ employees, you can enroll later without a penalty — usually within 8 months of that coverage ending.

Questions about Medicare enrollment?

Free Medicare Review

Frequently Asked Questions

When does Medicare enrollment start?
Your Initial Enrollment Period starts 3 months before the month you turn 65 and ends 3 months after — a 7-month window.
When does AEP begin?
The Annual Enrollment Period runs October 15 – December 7 every year, when you can join, switch, or drop a Medicare Advantage or Part D plan.
Can I delay Part B?
Yes, if you have active employer coverage from a company with 20 or more employees. Without it, delaying can trigger a lifetime penalty.
What if I miss my Initial Enrollment Period?
You may need to wait for the General Enrollment Period (January 1 – March 31) and could face lifetime late-enrollment penalties.
